source.title: Expanding the DNA editing toolbox: Novel lambda integrase variants targeting microalgal and human genome sequences

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Lambda integrase is efficient for large DNA integration but limited by intrinsic sequence specificity to cognate attB sites. The goal is to engineer variants targeting pseudosites in microalgae (Phaeodactylum tricornutum, Nannochloropsis oceanica) and human genomes to expand the DNA editing toolbox. Initial attempts to target microalgae in vivo failed due to DNA uptake and expression issues, but a human pseudosite was successfully targeted.

section_gists.heading: Abstract
section_gists.gist: Directed evolution of lambda integrase variants targeting microalgal and human pseudosites. Variants show >1000-fold specificity switch in vitro. A human genomic site was efficiently targeted. Microalgae targeting not yet achieved.
process_signals: Directed evolution used to alter specificity
methods: In vivo targeting in human cells

section_gists.heading: Introduction
section_gists.gist: Microalgae are promising cell factories but genome engineering is needed. SSRs like lambda integrase enable large DNA integration but are limited by sequence specificity. Engineering can broaden specificity. Int-h/218 is a cofactor-independent variant used as starting point.
process_signals: Problem: limited targeting range of recombinases
Constraint: need for homology to endogenous target motifs
Hypothesis: directed evolution can alter specificity
failures_or_limits: Conventional strain engineering has limited success

section_gists.heading: Library generation and selection protocol
section_gists.gist: Random mutagenesis of Int C3 using GeneMorph II. Library ligated into selection vectors with attPhae2 and attPhae2L sites. Two rounds of selection with increasing ampicillin (300-900 µg/ml then 12-18 mg/ml). C4 from round 1, C5 from round 2.
process_signals: Selection for recombination of attPhae2 x attPhae2L
Two rounds of selection with increasing stringency
methods: In vivo selection in E. coli (ampicillin resistance)

section_gists.heading: Recombination assay using in vitro translated proteins
section_gists.gist: In vitro transcription/translation (PURExpress) of integrase variants. Intramolecular recombination assayed by adding plasmid substrates (attB, attNanno, attHNanno, attPhae2) and detecting products by PCR.
process_signals: Initial screening of variants using IVT proteins

section_gists.heading: Recombination assay using purified proteins
section_gists.gist: Purified integrase (17 nM) incubated with plasmid substrates (1.3 nM) for 1 h. Real-time PCR quantification of recombined product using standard curve. End-point PCR also performed.

section_gists.heading: Molecular dynamics simulations
section_gists.gist: MD simulations of wild-type and mutant integrase tetramer-DNA complexes using Amber18. 250 ns conventional MD and 500 ns accelerated MD. Analysis of interactions between N99, H329 and DNA.
process_signals: Focus on N99 and H329 interactions
metrics: Simulation time (250 ns conventional, 500 ns aMD)

section_gists.heading: Selection of λ integrase variants with altered specificities
section_gists.gist: Int C3 showed ~22-fold improved recombination of attPhae2 over Int-h/218. Directed evolution yielded C4 and C5. C6 (C4 + H329R) showed additive effect. N99D identified as key specificity determinant. Purified C6 showed >1000-fold switch for attNanno1 and attHNanno.
process_signals: Int C3 as starting point due to improved activity on attPhae2
N99D mutation crucial for specificity switch
C6 showed >1000-fold switch for attNanno1 and attHNanno
Reduced activity on attB for C4 and C6
methods: In vitro recombination assay (IVT and purified proteins)
failures_or_limits: C4 and C6 had reduced efficiency on attB
Some variants lost activity on original substrate

section_gists.heading: Molecular dynamics simulations
section_gists.gist: MD simulations showed N99 interacts with guanine in attB; N99D loses this interaction. In attNanno (cytosine), N99D forms stable network with K95 and DNA. H329R forms salt bridge with D351, stabilizing tetramer. Combined mutations explain specificity switch.
process_signals: Rationalization of N99D and H329R roles
N99D enables interaction with cytosine in variant sites
H329R stabilizes tetramer via D351 salt bridge

section_gists.heading: In situ targeting of genomic attHNanno by Int-C7 resulting in delivery of functional payloads
section_gists.gist: Int-C7 with NLS expressed in human cells. Co-transfection with target vector carrying attHNannoL and payload (GFP, puroR). PCR and sequencing confirmed integration at attHNanno on chr11. Southern blot showed single-copy integration in 3/4 subclones. GFP expression detected. Intermolecular recombination preferred over intramolecular.
process_signals: Int-C7 (C3+N99D+H329R) used for human cell targeting
Surprising preference for intermolecular over intramolecular recombination
failures_or_limits: Intramolecular recombination less efficient than expected

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: Engineered integrase variants show specificity switch. Microalgae targeting failed likely due to DNA uptake/expression issues. Human attHNanno site successfully targeted; may be a safe harbor. N99D and H329R recapitulate natural HK022 integrase specificity. MD explains interactions. Future work: improve efficiency, reduce off-targets, target microalgae.
process_signals: N99D and H329R key for specificity

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: Directed evolution can alter lambda integrase specificity towards non-cognate sites.
N99D and H329R mutations are key for specificity switch, recapitulating natural HK022 integrase.
Computational modeling can rationalize observed specificity changes.
Engineered integrases can target human genomic pseudosites but microalgae targeting requires overcoming delivery barriers.
uncertainties_to_resolve: Why did microalgae targeting fail? (DNA uptake, expression, chromatin?)
What is the efficiency of integration in human cells?
Can further engineering improve activity and specificity?
Will the approach work in other organisms?

events.summary: Intrinsic sequence specificities of recombinases limit their use to DNA sequences with sufficient homology to endogenous target motifs.
Extensive engineering is required to broaden applicability and robustness of recombinases.
Directed evolution of lambda integrase variants targeting exogenous sequences in Phaeodactylum tricornutum and Nannochloropsis oceanica.
Library generated from C3-INT-HA using GeneMorph II Random Mutagenesis kit and ligated into selection vectors with attPhae2 and attPhae2L sites.
In vitro recombination assay using in vitro translated proteins to test integrase variants on various substrate pairs.
methods_or_tools: PURExpress In Vitro Protein Synthesis Kit

events.summary: Recombination assay using purified proteins with real-time PCR quantification of recombined product.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers abstract, introduction, methods for library generation and in vitro assays, and start of results. Extracted events focus on problem statement, hypothesis, experimental design, and key experimental steps with metrics.

events.summary: Random mutagenesis of Int C3 and two rounds of selection yielded variants C4 and C5 with potentially improved activity on attPhae2 x attPhae2L.
C4 and C5 showed potential improved activity over C3 for recombination of attNanno1 x attNanno1L.
N99D mutation identified as potentially responsible for specificity switch, based on proximity to DNA and prior reports.
Mutational analysis confirmed N99D key for specificity: N99D addition improved activity, D99N reversion reduced efficiency on attNanno substrates.
Int C7 designed with minimal mutations (N99D, H329R) plus C3 core, showing 345-fold and 1097-fold improvement for attNanno1 and attHNanno over C3, and >1000-fold specificity switch.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers directed evolution, variant characterization, and key mutation identification.

events.summary: MD simulations show N99D mutation enables interaction with cytosine in variant sites, explaining specificity switch.
numbers_or_metrics: interaction stable for >75% of simulation time

events.summary: Int-C7 with NLS expressed in human cells and used for genomic targeting of attHNanno.
PCR and sequencing confirmed left and right recombination junctions at genomic attHNanno.
Int-C7 catalyzed intermolecular recombination more efficiently than intramolecular, contrary to expectations.
Southern blot showed single-copy payload integration in 3/4 CL6 subclones, and GFP expression was homogeneous and sustained.
numbers_or_metrics: single copy integration in 3 out of 4 CL6 subclones

events.summary: Bioinformatics analysis suggests attHNanno locus is a safe harbor site favorable for transgene expression.
numbers_or_metrics: pseudogene HNRPA1P60 positioned about 40 kb upstream

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers MD simulation analysis, in situ targeting experiments in human cells, and bioinformatics characterization of the integration site.

events.summary: Bioinformatic analysis suggests attHNanno is a safe harbor that can now be targeted by the orthogonal Int C7 enzyme.

Identical selection of these amino acids in this study both further confirms their role in target site discrimination and illustrates how readily directed evolution can recapitulate natural selection.

MD simulations provide additional insight into the orthogonal phenotypes observed in this study. A favourable network of interactions promotes binding and activity of Int variants comprising N99D in the DNA core binding domain to substrates with the cytosine to guanine transversion.

H329 in the Int catalytic domain is distal to bound DNA. Mutation to R329 potentially introduces up to 4 intra-subunit salt bridges that could stabilize tetramer binding to DNA and favour recombination of non-cognate sequences.
RMCE is limited by the availability of pseudo sites and cognate enzymes targeting these.
The highly active C7 Int variant along with its orthogonal att site potentially raises the number of genes that can be targeted.
The odds of identifying appropriate pseudo-sites will also be improved by searching for flanking motifs orthogonal to C3 and C7 and subsequent combined use of these enzymes in a dual RMCE reaction.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains discussion and analysis of results, including bioinformatic analysis, MD simulation insights, and future hypotheses for expanding targeting capabilities.
This chunk contains only reference citations and no research process events.

evidence_bank.quote: intrinsic sequence specificities curtail their use to DNA sequences with sufficient homology to endogenous target motifs
Extensive engineering is therefore required to broaden applicability and robustness.

we describe the directed evolution of novel lambda integrase variants capable of editing exogenous target sequences identified in the diatom Phaeodactylum tricornutum and the algae Nannochloropsis oceanica
The integrase library was generated from C3-INT-HA pET22b(+) with GeneMorph II Random Mutagenesis kit
The library was ligated into pINT selection vectors comprising attPhae2 and attPhae2Lsites
Intramolecular recombination was then assayed by adding 10 ng relevant plasmid substrate
using PURExpress® In Vitro Protein Synthesis Kit
Recombinant integrase (17nM) was incubated with relevant plasmid substrate (1.3nM)
real-time PCR quantification with 100nM of primers 5 and 6

Random mutagenesis of Int C3 followed by 2 rounds of selection yielded two hits, C4 and C5 (Table 1) with potentially improved activity over Int C3 for recombination using attPhae2 x attPhae2L DNA substrates
both showed potential improved activity over Int C3 for recombination of attNanno1 x attNanno1L (Fig 3B)

highlighted N99D as potentially responsible for the observed specificity switch phenotype based on the proximity of N99 to the DNA substrate in the crystal structure and prior reports highlighting this residue as a specificity determinant

recombination by Int-h/218 + N99D and Int C3 + N99D was potentially improved over Int-h/218 and Int C3 (Fig 5). Conversely, the D99N reversion in Int C4 and Int C6 potentially led to reduced efficiency of recombination.

we generated a construct termed Int C7 (Table 1) comprising the expected minimal set of mutations required for improved recombination of the novel attNanno1 and attHNanno substrates (N99D and H329R) in addition to the core C3 mutations. Expressed and purified C7 showed 345-fold improvement over C3 for recombination of attNanno1 substrates and 1097-fold improvement for recombination of HTN substrates
This large network of interactions was observed to be stable for >75% of the simulation time and is likely a key determinant of the observed specificity switch.
Transfection into either HEK293ft or HT1080 cells following by Western blotting indicated stable Int C7 NLS expression at levels comparable to Int C3-NLS
The results showed that with 750ng and 1000ng of transfected target vector, both junctions can be generated by nested PCR, which was verified by PCR product sequencing
This indicates that contrary to expectations on thermodynamic grounds, Int-C7 surprisingly catalyzed intermolecular recombination more efficiently than intramolecular recombination
Southern analysis indicated single copy payload integration into attHNanno in 3 out of 4 CL6 subclones
transgene expression (GFP) from this targeted human locus was homogeneous and sustained in the three CL6 subclones
Bioinformatics analysis of the genomic attHNanno locus (chr11:110,828,911) shows the target sequence to be located in a gene-deserted region
these findings indicated that the locus can be considered a safe harbor site favorable for transgene expression
Bioinformatic analysis suggests attHNanno is a safe harbor that can now be targeted by the orthogonal Int C7 enzyme.

Identical selection of these amino acids in this study both further confirms their role in target site discrimination and illustrates how readily directed evolution can recapitulate natural selection.

MD simulations provide additional insight into the orthogonal phenotypes observed in this study. A favourable network of interactions promotes binding and activity of Int variants comprising N99D in the DNA core binding domain to substrates with the cytosine to guanine transversion.

H329 in the Int catalytic domain is distal to bound DNA. Mutation to R329 potentially introduces up to 4 intra-subunit salt bridges that could stabilize tetramer binding to DNA and favour recombination of non-cognate sequences.
RMCE is limited by the availability of pseudo sites and cognate enzymes targeting these.
The highly active C7 Int variant along with its orthogonal att site potentially raises the number of genes that can be targeted.
The odds of identifying appropriate pseudo-sites will also be improved by searching for flanking motifs orthogonal to C3 and C7 and subsequent combined use of these enzymes in a dual RMCE reaction.
The combined H329R and N99D mutations present in C7 therefore result in loss of the N99-DG8 interaction but improved R329-D351 interaction (attB-Int $^{C7}$ complex, Fig 10, S4 and S6A Figs).
However, the H329R mutation resulted in an R329 –D351 salt bridge interaction that was stable for 60% of the simulation time for the attB-Int $^{C3\_H329R}$ complex (S4 and S5B Figs).
The roles of the selected N99D and H329R mutations were further investigated using molecular dynamics (MD) simulations.

Based on results highlighting a potential key role for the N99D mutation in observed phenotype, we generated a construct termed Int C7 (Table 1) comprising the expected minimal set of mutations required for improved recombination of the novel attNanno1 and attHNanno substrates (N99D and H329R) in addition to the core C3 mutations.

Replacement of N99 to D99 (attB-Int $^{C7}$ and attB-Int $^{C3\_N99D}$ complexes), results in clear loss of the interaction with DG8, possibly explaining lack of activity of variants comprising N99D mutation on attB substrate (Fig 10, S4 and S5A Figs).
These were tested again along with a further variant termed C6, comprising all the mutations present in C4 in addition to the H329R mutation identified in C5 (Table 1).
A favourable network of interactions promotes binding and activity of Int variants comprising N99D in the DNA core binding domain to substrates with the cytosine to guanine transversion.
These results highlight a potential key role of the N99D mutation for the observed specificity switch of the C4 and C6 Int variants.
We therefore generated a panel of constructs to interrogate the N99D mutation.
INT218 N99D C3 N99D C4 D99N C6 D99N Blank NTC
These comprised Int-h/218 + N99D, Int C3 + N99D, Int C4 + D99N reversion, and Int C6 + D99N reversion.
(A) $attB-Int^{C7}$ (B) $attB^{DC8}$ -Int, DNA\_MUT: G8C, C22G.
In addition to these interactions, the R329-D351 interaction was observed for \~60% of the simulation time in the attB $^{DC8}$ -Int $^{C7}$ complex (S4 and S7A Figs).
No significant differences were observed for the R329-D351 interaction in the attB $^{DC8}$ -Int $^{C3\_H329R}$ complex compared to attB-Int $^{C3\_H329R}$ (S7B Fig).

highlighted N99D as potentially responsible for the observed specificity switch phenotype based on the proximity of N99 to the DNA substrate in the crystal structure and prior reports highlighting this residue as a specificity determinant $[37-39]$ .
(A) $attB^{DC8}-Int^{C7}$ (B) $attB^{DC8}-Int^{C3-H329R}$ .
(A) attB-Int (B) $attB-Int^{C3-H329R}$ .
When tested using attNanno x attNannoL substrates, recombination by Int-h/218 + N99D and Int C3 + N99D was potentially improved over Int-h/218 and Int C3 (Fig 5).
When DG8 in attB is replaced with cytosine (DC8) as present in attNanno, no interaction is observed between this base and N99 of Int (attB $^{DC8}$ -Int complex, Fig 10, S6B Fig).
Conversely, the D99N reversion in Int C4 and Int C6 potentially led to reduced efficiency of recombination.

The thermal cycling parameters used for primary PCRs from transfected and puromycin selected parental cells were as follows: initial denaturation at 95°C for 2 min, 35 cycles of denaturation at 95°C for 1 min, annealing at 57°C for 1 min and extension at 72°C for 1 min 10 sec (left junction) and 1 min 40 sec (right junction), and a final step of 72°C for 5 min.

Wild-type and variant integrase genes were amplified using INF-INT-nde1F and INF-INT-HA-ecoR1R (95°C 5mins, 95°C 5s, 55°C 20s, 72°C 1min, 25 cycles), cut with Nde1 and EcoR1, and ligated into similarly cut pET22 vecor.

The available crystal structure (pdb: 1Z1G [27]) of lambda integrase tetramer bound to a DNA holiday junction (excluding the integrase residues 1 to 74 and mutating the Phe342 back to wildtype Tyr342) was used to generate several mutant models of lambda integrase—DNA complexes.
Genomic editing in non-bacterial genomes is enabled by an engineered co-factor independent integrase variant (Int-h/218) $[23–26]$ .

Repetition of this experiment using a 10-fold lower final concentration of purified integrase and DNA substrate (Fig 9) led to similar results, with a 943-fold change in specificity for C6 recombination of attNanno1 versus attB substrates and a 1057-fold change in specificity for recombination of attHNanno substrates.
This represents a 3925-fold change in specificity for C6 recombination of attNanno1 versus attB substrates and a 5063-fold change in specificity for recombination of attHNanno substrates.
Conversely, C6 and C7 respectively recombined attB x attBL 13 and 6.3-fold less efficiently than C3.
Expressed and purified C7 showed 345-fold improvement over C3 for recombination of attNanno1 substrates and 1097-fold improvement for recombination of HTN substrates (Fig 8A).
results in Fig 6 show enhanced recombination of attNanno1 substrates by C4 (10-fold improved over C3) and C6 (28-fold improved over C3).
C4 was selected from the first round with 900 $\mu$ g/ml ampicillin while C5 was selected from the second round with 18 mg/ml ampicillin.
The library was ligated into the selection vector for the next round of selection under more stringent conditions (12mg/ml–18 mg/ml).

The library was ligated into pINT selection vectors comprising attPhae2 and attPhae2Lsites after Nde1/EcoR1 restriction, followed by electroporation into TG1 cells and plated on selection plates containing increasing amounts of ampicillin (300–900 $\mu$ g/ml).
Specificity determinants for bacteriophage Hong Kong 022 integrase: analysis of mutants with relaxed core-binding specificities.
Mutation to R329 potentially introduces up to 4 intra-subunit salt bridges that could stabilize tetramer binding to DNA and favour recombination of non-cognate sequences.
We screened individual cell clones from co-transfections and identified positive lines such as CL6, which showed the predicted junction product in the primary PCR as exemplified in Fig 13.
In the case of C7, significant fold difference was only observed for the attHNanno substrate with a 915-fold switch in specificity.

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Lambda integrase is efficient for large DNA integration but limited by intrinsic sequence specificity to cognate attB sites. The goal is to engineer variants targeting pseudosites in microalgae and human genomes to expand the DNA editing toolbox.

canonical_events.summary: Intrinsic sequence specificities of recombinases limit their use to DNA sequences with sufficient homology to endogenous target motifs.
Extensive engineering is required to broaden applicability and robustness of recombinases.
Directed evolution of lambda integrase variants targeting exogenous sequences in Phaeodactylum tricornutum and Nannochloropsis oceanica.

Library generated from C3-INT-HA using GeneMorph II Random Mutagenesis kit and ligated into selection vectors with attPhae2 and attPhae2L sites. Two rounds of selection with increasing ampicillin (300-900 µg/ml then 12-18 mg/ml) yielded variants C4 and C5.
In vitro recombination assay using in vitro translated proteins showed C4 and C5 had potentially improved activity over C3 for recombination of attNanno1 x attNanno1L.
N99D mutation identified as potentially responsible for specificity switch, based on proximity to DNA and prior reports.
Mutational analysis confirmed N99D key for specificity: N99D addition improved activity on attNanno substrates, D99N reversion reduced efficiency.
Int C7 designed with minimal mutations (N99D, H329R) plus C3 core, showing 345-fold and 1097-fold improvement for attNanno1 and attHNanno over C3, and >1000-fold specificity switch.
MD simulations show N99D mutation enables interaction with cytosine in variant sites, explaining specificity switch. H329R forms salt bridge with D351, stabilizing tetramer.
key_numbers: interaction stable for >75% of simulation time

canonical_events.summary: Int-C7 with NLS expressed in human cells and used for genomic targeting of attHNanno. PCR and sequencing confirmed left and right recombination junctions at genomic attHNanno on chr11.
Int-C7 catalyzed intermolecular recombination more efficiently than intramolecular, contrary to expectations.
Southern blot showed single-copy payload integration in 3/4 CL6 subclones, and GFP expression was homogeneous and sustained.
key_numbers: single copy integration in 3 out of 4 CL6 subclones

canonical_events.summary: Bioinformatics analysis suggests attHNanno locus is a safe harbor site favorable for transgene expression.
key_numbers: pseudogene HNRPA1P60 positioned about 40 kb upstream

canonical_events.summary: Microalgae targeting failed likely due to DNA uptake/expression issues. RMCE limited by availability of pseudo sites and cognate enzymes.

edges.reason: Problem of limited specificity motivates hypothesis that engineering can broaden applicability.
Hypothesis leads to design of directed evolution targeting microalgal sequences.
Design is implemented by generating library and selecting variants.
Selected variants C4 and C5 are tested in vitro for improved activity.
Activity data leads to identification of N99D as key mutation.
Hypothesis about N99D is tested by mutational analysis.
Confirmation of N99D role enables design of minimal variant C7.
MD simulations provide mechanistic explanation for specificity switch of C7.
C7 variant is used for genomic targeting in human cells.
Junction detection reveals unexpected preference for intermolecular recombination.
Integration is validated by Southern blot and GFP expression.
Successful integration leads to bioinformatic characterization of the locus as safe harbor.
Initial goal of microalgae targeting was not achieved due to delivery barriers.

must_cover_points: Directed evolution strategy targeting microalgal pseudosites
Selection of C4 and C5 variants
Identification of N99D as key specificity determinant
Design of minimal variant C7 with N99D and H329R
Quantitative specificity switch (>1000-fold) for attNanno1 and attHNanno
MD simulations explaining N99D and H329R roles
Successful genomic targeting of attHNanno in human cells
Validation of single-copy integration and transgene expression
Failure to target microalgae in vivo
